message:
fix(termwiz): clamp Windows console coordinates to i16 range

Multiple locations cast usize terminal dimensions and cursor positions
directly to i16 for Windows Console API calls. On terminals wider than
32767 columns (theoretically possible with high-DPI or remote sessions),
these would silently truncate to negative values, corrupting the
console state.

- terminal/windows.rs: clamp cols/rows to i16::MAX before cast (2 sites)
- render/windows.rs: clamp cursor_x/cursor_y, scroll_count, first_row,
  and region_size to i16::MAX with saturating_add for offset arithmetic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -352,8 +352,8 @@ impl ConsoleOutputHandle for OutputHandle {
                 self.handle.as_raw_handle() as *mut _,
                 res.as_mut_ptr(),
                 COORD {
-                    X: cols as i16,
-                    Y: rows as i16,
+                    X: cols.min(i16::MAX as usize) as i16,
+                    Y: rows.min(i16::MAX as usize) as i16,
                 },
                 COORD { X: 0, Y: 0 },
                 &mut read_region,
@@ -387,8 +387,8 @@ impl ConsoleOutputHandle for OutputHandle {
                 self.handle.as_raw_handle() as *mut _,
                 buffer.as_ptr(),
                 COORD {
-                    X: cols as i16,
-                    Y: rows as i16,
+                    X: cols.min(i16::MAX as usize) as i16,
+                    Y: rows.min(i16::MAX as usize) as i16,
                 },
                 COORD { X: 0, Y: 0 },
                 &mut write_region,
